index 0167e83..cfdaf04 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,36 +1,35 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 8 actions (Day 56)
-- Cash before actions: $502,019; after starting R&D tier1: $335,352. Active small subscribers: 9,368 (S2 9,338; S1 30). Enterprise subscribed seats: 0. Active stored MRR: ~$607.3k/mo (S2 ~$606.9k). Open issues: 762 (S2 avg age ~0.008d, effectively same-day; S1 3 older). No outage/overload; peak daily usage ~298k vs tier3 2.5M.
-- Week 8 result from $4k/day S2 content ads: 3,515 paid S2 leads (~502/day) + ~484 S2 network leads; paid conversion ~86.6%, network conversion ~88%; plan mix ~67% A / 33% B / tiny C. Subscription receipts $182.2k vs total weekly costs ~$77.4k, cash rose $104.9k before new R&D. CAC remains immediately cash-positive (content spend+lead fee <$9/lead; first cash per paid sub ~$46).
-- Day56 actions now in force:
-  - Prices A=$49, B=$99, C=$199. Tiers A=2, B=3, C=4. Quotas raised to A=400, B=1500, C=5000 to reduce cap friction. Recurring promo $0. Lead promo unchanged: S2 $20 only; others $0. Ads strength 0.
-  - S2 content_marketing ads doubled from $4k/day to $8k/day. `set_targeted_ad_spend` is full replacement; only content/S2 is funded.
-  - Spend: global ops $500/day + targeted S2 ops $3,500/day (total ops $4k/day) to protect support at higher volume. Dev held global $1k/day + targeted S2 $1k/day (total dev $2k/day). Capacity tier3 retained.
-  - Started R&D tier1 (project_id t1_1): cost $166,667 paid Day56; sampled completion Day86 (30 days) and quality boost +0.0289 (worse/slower than mean but still useful). Do not start more R&D until cash recovers, likely >$800k-$1M; tier2 costs $333,333.
-  - Posted Day56 social: expanded plan headroom/quota alerts, same-day blocker triage, ROI/reliability focus in tight budgets.
+## Current strategy after Week 9 actions (Day 63)
+- Day63 before actions: cash $641,016; after starting R&D tier2 cash $307,683. Active individual subs 16,894 (S2 16,864; S1 30). Enterprise subscribed seats 0. Active stored MRR ~$1.098M/mo. Open issues 1,348; S2 issues avg age only ~0.056d, but social still has many S2 quota-wall complaints. No outage/overload; last-week peak daily usage ~1.34M vs tier3 2.5M.
+- Week 8->9 results from $8k/day S2 content ads: 7,864 paid S2 leads (~1,123/day) + ~938 S2 network leads; paid conversion ~87.7%, network conversion ~87.6%; paid plan mix ~66%A / 33%B / tiny C. Subscription receipts $445k vs weekly costs ~$139k, cash rose ~$306k despite higher ops/dev. CAC still immediately cash-positive: ad+lead cost ~<$9/sub, first payment avg ~$46 before this week’s price change.
+- Day63 actions now in force:
+  - Prices raised for NEW customers to A=$59, B=$119, C=$249. Existing subscriptions appear not repriced. Tiers A=2, B=3, C=4. Quotas raised to A=600, B=2500, C=8000 to answer quota complaints. Recurring promo $0. Lead promo still S2=$20 only; others $0. Ads strength remains 0.
+  - S2 content_marketing ads doubled again to $16k/day. `set_targeted_ad_spend` is full replacement; only content_marketing/S2 is funded.
+  - Spend: global ops $500/day + targeted S2 ops $7,000/day (total ops $7.5k/day). Global dev $1,000/day + targeted S2 dev $3,000/day (total dev $4k/day). Capacity increased to tier4 (8M units, $4k/day) to protect doubled ad scale and possible E3 acceptances.
+  - R&D: tier1 in progress t1_1, completes Day86, sampled boost +0.0289. Started tier2 t2_1 on Day63, cost $333,333, sampled completion Day93 (30d) and boost +0.0619. Do not start tier3 unless cash after week is safely >$1.0M or growth remains strongly cash-positive; tier3 cost $500k.
+  - Posted Day63 social: announced A600/B2500/C8000 headroom, burst-safe workflow alerts, expanded support/eval capacity.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- 22 open E3 leads totaling ~22,010 seats; all latest open turns are from agent after Day56 replies, so no current reply needed. Check enterprise_turns every week before advancing.
-- New Day50-56 E3 leads (11,393 seats: 7966, 8684, 8685, 8686, 8687, 9978, 10356, 10357, 11408, 11410, 11411) were replied Day56 with C $69 / B $49.
-- Older E3 leads from Day49 are still awaiting customer response; E3 info says avg response ~23.8 days, so no acceptances yet is not alarming. 4991 remains special small pilot/counter at C $42.02 / B $39; others C $69 / B $49.
-- E3 unit economics: noisy estimate WTP ~$104/seat/mo, usage ~137.5 units/seat/day. At B49, model cost ~= $24.75/seat/mo; at C69, model cost ~= $49.50/seat/mo, so B has better margin and C is acceptable at $69. Avoid C prices in low $40s for huge seat counts except small strategic pilots/counters.
-- `enterprise_autoresponder.py` is registered and only responds when latest sender is customer/system. `capacity_autoscaler.py` is registered; manually verify enterprise inbox and capacity anyway.
+- IMPORTANT: The supposed weekly enterprise_autoresponder did NOT handle Day57-63 leads automatically; 20 latest system/customer turns were awaiting manual response. Always manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; do not rely on scripts/registration.
+- Day63 manual enterprise responses: replied to all 20 latest system/customer turns totaling 26,608 seats. Standard new E3 offers remain C $69 / B $49. Accepted/counter-matched customer 5618 (757 seats) with B $27.67 as first strategic low-margin pilot. After actions, latest customer/system awaiting response count = 0.
+- Total open E3 pipeline after Day63: 41 leads / ~47,861 seats. Older Day49/Day56 leads are still waiting on customers; E3 avg response previously estimated ~23.8d, so responses may start soon. Check for acceptances/counters every week before next-week.
+- E3 unit economics: noisy WTP ~$104/seat/mo, usage ~137.5 units/seat/day. At B49, model cost ~= $24.75/seat/mo; at C69, cost ~= $49.50/seat/mo, so B has better margin and C acceptable at $69. B27.67 is only small positive margin and should be rare/strategic. Avoid C prices in low $40s for large seat counts.
 
-## Day 63 priorities
-1. Run `report.py` and `analyze_week.py`. Evaluate $8k/day S2 ad scale: leads/day, leads per dollar, conversion, plan mix, first payments, support issue age, and net cash. If paid conversion stays >75-80% and cashflow remains strong, consider $10k-$12k/day; if lead efficiency collapses or support ages, step back to $4k-$6k.
-2. Watch renewals and downgrades closely. Next 7 days had ~$136k scheduled active billings pre-churn; next 28 days roughly most of the ~$607k MRR. Full-price retention validates the high-growth model.
-3. Check all enterprise turns immediately. Respond to customer counters/acceptance-required messages before advancing. If E3 seats accept, inspect actual usage and margins; tier3 is fine for current small base, but tier4 if usage/fallback approaches >2.5M/day.
-4. Monitor support/social after quota raise and ops increase. If open issue avg age >1 day or support-delay posts recur, raise targeted S2 ops again before further ad scale.
-5. R&D: tier1 completes Day86. Consider tier2 once cash is safely >$800k-$1M and S2 growth remains cash-positive.
+## Day 70 priorities
+1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, and query latest enterprise turns immediately. Confirm no customer/system latest enterprise messages before advancing; respond within the same week.
+2. Evaluate the aggressive $16k/day S2 content scale and price increase: paid leads/day, leads per dollar, conversion after A59/B119/C249, plan mix, first-payment cash, and support/social. If paid conversion stays >70-75% and cashflow remains strong, keep $16k or consider cautious $20k-$24k; if conversion collapses or lead efficiency falls below ~0.07 leads/$, cut back to $8k-$12k and/or revisit price/promo.
+3. Watch support and quota sentiment closely. If S2 open issue avg age >0.5-1d or quota/support complaints continue, raise targeted ops further or slow ad growth. Tier4 should handle expected load; check service_day usage vs 8M.
+4. Renewals/downgrades: existing next-7-day billings before churn were only ~$111k but next-28-day scheduled rev ~$948k. Measure retention at full price and downgrade rate after quota increases.
+5. R&D/market: If Day70 cash is safely >$1M, consider tier3 R&D ($500k). If cash >$1.5M-$2M, consider tier3 plus a small number of market discovery attempts; but protect the cash-positive S2 engine first.
 
 ## Segment/unit-economics notes
-- S2 remains core growth engine. Info-level estimate WTP ~$179/mo, usage ~$231/day, q_min ~0.37, cap ~167k. Observed usage is far lower (~20-50 units/sub/day for A/B recently). With S2 $20 lead promo, first cash per new sub averages ~$46 and list MRR averages ~$65. Current delivered quality: S2 A~0.75, B~0.90, C~1.00.
+- S2 is still the core growth engine. Info estimate WTP ~$179/mo, usage ~$231/day, q_min ~0.37, cap ~167k. Observed usage remains much lower than estimate: last week S2 A avg ~25-110 units/day, B ~56-195/day, C ~100-217/day; near-cap users low after quota increase. Delivered quality on Day63 pre-actions: S2 A~0.778, B~0.934, C~1.037. New A59/B119 should be tested carefully but should remain acceptable if quality/quotas hold.
 - S1 is low value; no paid S1 ads/promos. S3 power users remain untargeted because high usage can be unprofitable on low plans.
-- E3 strategic partners are generated by S2 network/reputation. Long-cycle response is normal; keep offers margin-aware.
+- Network effects from S2 generate E3 strategic leads. Long enterprise cycles are normal; keep offers margin-aware and use tier4+ capacity if seats accept.
 
-## Forecast assumptions submitted Day56
-- Base no-enterprise model after actions: $8k/day S2 content, conversion ~84-86%, plan mix ~67%A/33%B, network leads scaling with S2 base, retention ~92-95%, ops/dev/capacity as above, tier1 R&D cost already paid. No-enterprise rough cash: 1wk ~$0.60-$0.65M; 4wk ~$1.4M; 12wk ~$7M; 26wk ~$26M before future strategic changes.
-- Enterprise is main upside; ad saturation/support/renewal churn/competitors are downside. Day56 forecast to submit: 1wk point $670k CI $500k-$1.15M; 4wk point $1.8M CI $1.0M-$3.2M; 12wk point $8.0M CI $4.0M-$14.0M; 26wk point $32M CI $12M-$70M.
+## Forecast assumptions submitted Day63
+- After actions, model assumptions: $16k/day S2 content, price A59/B119/C249 with S2 $20 first-period promo, conversion uncertain ~70-85%, first payment avg ~$55-$60, network leads scale with active S2, monthly retention ~90-95%, fixed daily ops/dev/capacity ~$15.5k plus ads and compute, tier2 R&D cost already paid. Enterprise is the main upside; price-shock/ad saturation/support/competitors are downside.
+- Day63 forecasts to submit: 1wk point $1.0M CI $0.55M-$1.8M; 4wk point $3.8M CI $1.8M-$7.0M; 12wk point $24M CI $8M-$55M; 26wk point $75M CI $25M-$180M.
 
 ## Operating reminders / lessons
@@ -38,6 +37,6 @@
 - Query subscription statuses with `status IN ('subscribed','active')` defensively. Subscription table has `billing_day_mod30`, not `next_billing_day`; compute days until bill from current day mod 30. Config audit table is `config_overrides`, not `config_changes`.
 - Lead promotions reduce first billing only; `subscriptions.effective_price` stores list/ongoing price, while ledger first payments show promo-discounted cash.
-- Price changes do not appear to reprice older existing subscriptions; new Day43+ S2 subscriptions store A49/B99 list and pay A29/B79 first period.
+- Price changes do not appear to reprice older existing subscriptions; new subscriptions after Day63 should store A59/B119/C249 and pay A39/B99/C229 first period due S2 $20 lead promo.
 - `set_targeted_ad_spend` is a full replacement budget. Include only desired channel/group pairs; omitted previous spend is cleared.
 - API gotcha: use `nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})`, not `by_group=...`. Actions before a Python exception may already apply; verify config.
-- If editing registered scripts, re-run `./novamind-ope

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, random, statistics, math, json
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
cash0=nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger")['rows'][0]['cash']
# Existing small cohorts by exact price/start/billing mod
rows=nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day,COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status IN ('subscribed','active') AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2),s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
""")['rows']
base_cohorts=[]
for r in rows:
    base_cohorts.append({'n':float(r['n']), 'price':float(r['price']), 'mod':int(r['billing_day_mod30']), 'age':DAY-int(r['start_day']), 'group':r['group_id'], 'plan':r['plan'], 'newprice':False})
# Open enterprise threads: max seat count by customer, first lead day, latest offer price
ent_rows=nm.query("""
WITH last AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
mx AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) first_day FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT l.customer_id, c.group_id, mx.seats, mx.first_day, t.day latest_day, t.offer_json, t.sender
FROM last l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN mx ON mx.customer_id=l.customer_id JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id
WHERE t.closed=0
""")['rows']
open_ents=[]
for r in ent_rows:
    if r['group_id']!='E3': continue
    seats=float(r['seats'] or 0)
    if seats<=10: continue
    offer = r.get('offer_json') or ''
    price=49; plan='B'
    if '27.67' in offer:
        price=27.67; plan='B'; pilot=True
    else:
        pilot=False
        # assume customer usually picks B49 over C69; sometimes C for quality-sensitive
    open_ents.append({'cid':r['customer_id'], 'seats':seats, 'first_day':int(r['first_day'] or DAY), 'latest_day':int(r['latest_day'] or DAY), 'pilot':pilot})
print('cash0', cash0, 'small cohorts', len(base_cohorts), 'active', sum(c['n'] for c in base_cohorts), 'open ents', len(open_ents), 'ent seats', sum(e['seats'] for e in open_ents))

def pct(vals,p):
    vals=sorted(vals); return vals[int((len(vals)-1)*p)]

def simulate(days=84, seed=0):
    random.seed(seed)
    cash=float(cash0)
    cohorts=[c.copy() for c in base_cohorts]
    # run-level uncertain parameters after price increase and ad doubling
    eff=random.lognormvariate(math.log(0.135),0.20)  # paid leads/$ before saturation
    paid_base=16000*eff
    conv_base=max(0.55,min(0.92, random.gauss(0.80,0.065)))
    net_rate=max(0.006,min(0.014, random.gauss(0.010,0.002)))
    market_cap=max(130000, random.gauss(167000,20000))
    # plan mix after price/quotas
    mixA=max(0.58,min(0.82, random.gauss(0.70,0.055)))
    mixB=max(0.16,min(0.38, random.gauss(0.285,0.045)))
    if mixA+mixB>0.985: mixB=0.985-mixA
    monthly_ret=max(0.875,min(0.975, random.gauss(0.935,0.025)))
    # Enterprise acceptance probability and delays
    ent_events=[]
    for e in open_ents:
        if e['pilot']:
            p=max(0.45,min(0.90, random.gauss(0.70,0.10)))
            mean_resp=7
        else:
            p=max(0.12,min(0.50, random.gauss(0.30,0.08)))
            mean_resp=24
        if random.random()<p:
            age=max(0, DAY-e['first_day'])
            rem=max(1, int(random.gauss(mean_resp-age*0.55, 8)))
            # very stale threads can still take a few days
            if not e['pilot'] and age>18: rem=max(1, int(random.gauss(7,5)))
            ev_day=DAY+rem
            plan='B' if random.random()<0.72 else 'C'
            price=49.0 if plan=='B' else 69.0
            if e['pilot']:
                plan='B'; price=27.67
            ent_events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':e['seats'],'plan':plan,'price':price,'mod':ev_day%30})
    ent_active=[]
    # future enterprise leads from S2 network (responded weekly; closes after lag)
    future_ent_leads=[]
    for d in range(DAY+1, DAY+days+1):
        active=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts)
        # acquisition saturation/fatigue near S2 cap
        sat=max(0.05, 1-active/(market_cap*1.05))
        day_paid=max(0, random.gauss(paid_base, paid_base*0.22))*sat
        day_net=max(0, random.gauss(active*net_rate, max(8, active*net_rate*0.30)))*sat
        leads=day_paid+day_net
        # New E3 leads generated by S2 reputation/network, eventually get offers and may close
        e3_lambda=max(0, active*0.00020*sat)
        # approximate poisson via normal for speed
        e3_new=max(0, int(random.gauss(e3_lambda, max(1, math.sqrt(max(1,e3_lambda))))))
        for _ in range(e3_new):
            seats=max(300, random.gauss(1350,450))
            # offer next weekly check, then response lag
            if random.random()<max(0.10,min(0.42, random.gauss(0.27,0.08))):
                delay=max(10, int(random.gauss(27,10)))
                ev_day=d+delay
                plan='B' if random.random()<0.75 else 'C'; price=49.0 if plan=='B' else 69.0
                if ev_day <= DAY+days:
                    ent_events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':seats,'plan':plan,'price':price,'mod':ev_day%30})
        # conversion. R&D boosts after expected dates; support/quotas protect conversion.
        cv=conv_base
        if d>=86: cv+=0.015
        if d>=93: cv+=0.018
        cv=max(0.45,min(0.95, random.gauss(cv,0.045)))
        new_subs=leads*cv
        ma=max(0.50,min(0.88, random.gauss(mixA,0.035)))
        mb=max(0.10,min(0.45, random.gauss(mixB,0.030)))
        if ma+mb>0.99: mb=0.99-ma
        nA=new_subs*ma; nB=new_subs*mb; nC=max(0,new_subs-nA-nB)
        # first payments with S2 $20 lead promo; ongoing prices stored at list
        cash += nA*39 + nB*99 + nC*229
        cohorts.append({'n':nA,'price':59.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'A','newprice':True})
        cohorts.append({'n':nB,'price':119.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'B','newprice':True})
        if nC>0:
            cohorts.append({'n':nC,'price':249.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'C','newprice':True})
        # enterprise acceptances
        for ev in list(ent_events):
            if ev['day']==d:
                cash += ev['seats']*ev['price']
                ent_active.append({'seats':ev['seats'],'price':ev['price'],'plan':ev['plan'],'mod':d%30})
                ent_events.remove(ev)
        # renewals for small cohorts
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['age']>0 and c['mod']==d%30 and c['n']>0:
                ret=monthly_ret
                # new higher-price cohorts have a little more churn unless R&D complete
                if c.get('newprice'): ret -= 0.015
                if d>=86: ret += 0.010
                if d>=93: ret += 0.010
                if c['group']!='S2': ret=0.70
                ret=max(0.72,min(0.992, random.gauss(ret,0.025)))
                c['n'] *= ret
                # downgrade/revenue erosion (mainly B/C to A) beyond pure cancels
                rev_factor=1.0
                if c['group']=='S2' and c['plan'] in ('B','C'):
                    rev_factor=max(0.90,min(1.0, random.gauss(0.965,0.020)))
                cash += c['n']*c['price']*rev_factor
            c['age'] += 1
        # enterprise renewals
        for e in ent_active:
            if e['mod']==d%30:
                if random.random()<0.91:
                    cash += e['seats']*e['price']
                else:
                    e['seats']=0
        ent_active=[e for e in ent_active if e['seats']>0]
        # costs
        active=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts)
        ent_seats=sum(e['seats'] for e in ent_active)
        # Fixed daily actions: ad 16k, ops 7.5k, dev 4k, tier4 cap 4k
        cash -= (16000+7500+4000+4000)
        cash -= leads*0.5
        # compute: small usage grew with quotas; enterprise by assumed 137.5 units/seat/day
        cash -= active*max(0.22, random.gauss(0.32,0.06))
        cash -= sum(e['seats']*(0.006 if e['plan']=='B' else 0.012)*137.5 for e in ent_active)
        # occasional outage/support shock cost to cash via credits/churn modeled as direct penalty
        if random.random()<0.01:
            cash -= random.uniform(20000,100000)
    return cash, sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts), sum(c['n']*c['price'] for c in cohorts), sum(e['seats'] for e in ent_active)

for days in [7,28,84,182]:
    vals=[]; subs=[]; mrr=[]; ents=[]
    for seed in range(2000):
        v,s,m,e=simulate(days, seed+days*10000)
        vals.append(v); subs.append(s); mrr.append(m); ents.append(e)
    print(days, 'point/mean', round(statistics.mean(vals)), 'median', round(statistics.median(vals)), 'p025', round(pct(vals,0.025)), 'p10', round(pct(vals,0.10)), 'p90', round(pct(vals,0.90)), 'p975', round(pct(vals,0.975)), 'subs_med', round(statistics.median(subs)), 'mrr_med', round(statistics.median(mrr)), 'ent_med', round(statistics.median(ents)))

import json
import novamind_api as nm


def do(label, fn):
    try:
        res = fn()
        print(f"=== {label} ===")
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2) if not isinstance(res, str) else res)
        return res
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"*** ERROR in {label}: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
        return None

print("Day", nm.vars.current_day, "starting cash", nm.query("SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS cash FROM ledger").get('rows'))

# Pricing / packaging: S2 conversion is still very high and quota complaints persist.
# Raise new-customer ARPU while also increasing headroom so the price lift is paired with obvious value.
do('set_prices', lambda: nm.pricing.set_prices(A=59, B=119, C=249))
do('set_model_tiers', lambda: nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4))
do('set_usage_quotas', lambda: nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=600, B=2500, C=8000))
do('set_promotion', lambda: nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}))
do('set_lead_promotion', lambda: nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1": 0.0, "S2": 20.0, "S3": 0.0, "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0}))
do('set_ads_strength', lambda: nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}))

# S2 content ads remain immediately cash-positive; double again, while adding support/dev/capacity guardrails.
do('set_targeted_ad_spend', lambda: nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing": {"S2": 16000.0}}))
do('set_daily_spend', lambda: nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=500.0, development=1000.0))
do('set_targeted_ops_spend', lambda: nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 7000.0}))
do('set_targeted_dev_spend', lambda: nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 3000.0}))
do('set_capacity_tier', lambda: nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=4))

# Begin next near-term R&D tier now that the growth engine is safely cash-positive.
do('start_research_tier2', lambda: nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2))

# Enterprise: respond to every latest customer/system open turn. Keep standard E3 offers margin-aware;
# accept the one low-B counter as a strategic first enterprise pilot with modestly positive B-unit economics.
q = """
SELECT t.customer_id, c.group_id, t.sender, t.offer_json, t.message_text, t.seat_count AS seats
FROM enterprise_turns t
JOIN (
  SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid
  FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE closed=0
  GROUP BY customer_id
) last ON t.message_id = last.mid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.closed=0 AND t.sender IN ('customer','system')
ORDER BY t.day ASC, t.customer_id ASC
"""
rows = nm.query(q).get('rows', [])
deals = []
for r in rows:
    cid = int(r['customer_id'])
    g = r.get('group_id')
    sender = r.get('sender')
    price = None
    try:
        oj = json.loads(r.get('offer_json') or '{}')
        if isinstance(oj, dict) and 'price' in oj:
            price = float(oj['price'])
    except Exception:
        pass
    if g == 'E3':
        if sender == 'customer' and price is not None and price <= 30.0:
            offers = [["B", round(price, 2)]]
        elif sender == 'customer' and price is not None and price <= 45.0:
            offers = [["B", 39.0], ["C", 59.0]]
        else:
            offers = [["C", 69.0], ["B", 49.0]]
    elif g == 'E2':
        offers = [["C", 75.0], ["B", 55.0]]
    elif g == 'E1':
        offers = [["B", 18.0], ["A", 12.0]]
    else:
        offers = [["C", 69.0], ["B", 49.0]]
    deals.append([cid, offers])
print('enterprise deals prepared', len(deals), 'seat_sum', sum(int(r.get('seats') or 0) for r in rows))
if deals:
    do('send_enterprise_deal', lambda: nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals))

# Social message to counter recurring quota-wall posts and reinforce reliability/ROI.
social = "We’re raising plan headroom again (A 600/B 2500/C 8000 units/day), adding burst-safe workflow alerts, and expanding support + eval capacity so client deliverables don’t hit surprise hard stops."
do('post_social_media', lambda: nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=social))

print("Day", nm.vars.current_day, "ending cash", nm.query("SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS cash FROM ledger").get('rows'))
